notes:
A design-system pass — the whole app now draws its typography, weights, colours
and corners from one shared token set. Mostly invisible-by-design consistency,
with a real readability win. (See `docs/audits/design-system-audit.html`.)

**Fixes & polish**

- **Fine print is bigger and easier to read.** Counts, dates, badges,
  breadcrumbs and other small labels no longer dip to 10–12px — 13px is now the
  smallest text anywhere in the app.
- **Consistent type and weight on every screen.** The sidebar, tabs, editor,
  mentions, Home, Browse, settings and dialogs now share one type scale and a
  clean three-weight hierarchy, so nothing reads slightly-off from screen to
  screen. The app moved to Source Sans 3 so those weights are real rather than
  browser-synthesized.
- **Cleaner dark mode.** A batch of hard-coded light colours that used to leak
  through in dark mode — dialog backgrounds, banners, image controls, a
  selected-row highlight — now follow the theme correctly.
- **One consistent modal dim and a single 4px corner radius** across the app.

**Under the hood**

- Groundwork for upcoming theming and an optional font-size control: all
  typography is rem-based behind a dormant scale hook, and every colour resolves
  through a design token. A CSS lint now enforces the 13px floor, token-only
  colours, and the three bundled font-weights so the system can't silently
  regress.
